provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Land (Mortgage) Regulations, 2005

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Land Act, 1999 ; “ applicant ” means a person who makes a claim; “ possession application ” means an application for the possession of mortgaged land, including building and parts of buildings; “ Tribunal ” means the District Land and Housing Tribunal; “ variable mortgage interest rate ” means an interest rate of a mortgage, terms and conditions of which empower the mortgagee on giving due notice to alter or vary the rate of interest payable with respect to the mortgage during the currency of the term of the mortgage.

provisions.title: Forms
provisions.content: The Forms set out in the Schedule to these Regulations shall form part of the Land (Forms) Regulations, 2001 and shall replace the forms that relate to matters for which the forms made under these Regulations, apply. [ GN. No. ... of 2001] Part II – Conduct of the business of a mortgage

provisions.title: Reasonable steps to ascertain matrimonial status of applicant for mortgage
provisions.content: (1) Subject to section 114 of the Act, the following shall be taken for purposes of section 114 of the Act to be reasonable steps taken by a mortgagee to ascertain whether the applicant for a mortgage has a spouse or spouses— (a) a mortgagee shall require an applicant to sign a form stating that he or she is either married or not; (b) if the applicant states that he or she is married, requiring that applicant to state the names and address of his or her spouse or in the case of a male applicant , the names and addresses of his spouses if he has more than one; and (c) if the applicant states that he or she is not married and the mortgagee has reason to believe that, the statement might be incorrect, the mortgagee may require that applicant to produce an affidavit to the effect that the applicant is not married. (2) A mortgagee may take such other steps in addition to the steps set out in this regulation, as he considers necessary and desirable to satisfy himself as to the matrimonial status of an applicant and these steps shall be made known to the applicant when he or she applies for a mortgage.

provisions.title: Assent by a spouse to a mortgage other than a customary mortgage of a matrimonial home
provisions.content: (1) This regulation applies to a mortgage other than customary mortgage of a matrimonial home. (2) Where a matrimonial home is the subject of a mortgage, a mortgagee may satisfy himself that the assent of a spouse referred to in section 114 of the Act is an informed and genuine consent and this duty shall be deemed to have been complied with if— (a) the mortgagee has, in writing, advised the applicant for a mortgage that he or she should ensure that his or her spouse, or in the case of a male applicant with more than one spouse, his spouses, receive independent advice on the terms and conditions of the mortgage which is being applied for; and (b) the spouse or spouses, as the case may be, provide a signed and witnessed document to the effect that they have received independent advice on the mortgage which is being applied for and have understood and assented to the terms and conditions of the mortgage or that they have, notwithstanding the advice from the firm, waived their opportunity to take independent advice. (3) A mortgagee may take such other steps in addition to the steps set out in this regulation, as he considers necessary and desirable to satisfy himself that the assent of the spouse or spouses is informed and genuine.

provisions.title: Assent by a spouse to a customary mortgage of a matri­monial home
provisions.content: (1) Where a matrimonial home is a subject of a customary mortgage, a mortgagee may satisfy himself that the assent of a spouse referred to in section 114 of the Act is an informed and genuine consent and this duty shall be deemed to have been complied with if— (a) the mortgagee has explained to the spouse of an applicant for a mortgage, or in the case of a male applicant with more than one spouse, to his spouses, in the presence of an independent person, the terms and conditions of the mortgage which is being applied for; and (b) the spouse or spouses, as the case may be, provide a signed and witnessed document or other clear evidence to the effect that they have received independent advice on the mortgage which is being applied for and have understood and assented to the terms and conditions of the mortgage. (2) In this regulation an “independent person” means a person who is unrelated to, or who is not involved in any joint business or commercial enterprise with, or is not a tenant or the landlord of the applicant for a mortgage.

provisions.title: Notice of the variation of the interest payable under a mortgage
provisions.content: A mortgage intending to exercise his powers under section 120(1) of the Act to vary the rate at which interest is payable on a variable mortgage interest rate may exercise this power by serving a notice on the mortgagor which sets out clearly and in a manner, from language likely to be understood by the mortgagor— (a) the new interest rate which is to be paid; (b) the alternatives, if the mortgagee is offering any, to paying an increased sum by way of interest in cases where a new interest rate is to be higher than the interest rate which is varied; and (c) the responsibility of the mortgagor to take such action as he or she is advised by the notice to take, to ensure that the new interest rate is paid to the mortgagee. Part III – Mortgage dispute 1 – General

provisions.title: Application of this Part
provisions.content: This Part shall apply to any dispute by a mortgagee or mortgagor or any person having interest in any mortgaged land, in respect of the following matters: (a) payment of monies secured by the mortgage; (b) sale of the mortgaged land; (c) delivery of possession of the mortgaged land to the mortgagee by the mortgagor or any other person who is or is alleged to be in possession of the mortgaged land; (d) the discharge of the land from the security; or (e) delivery of possession of the land by the mortgagee to the mortgage or; (f) an action by a mortgagor for an account by a receiver of how the receiver has applied any moneys received under section 128 of the Act ; (g) an action by a/mortgagor against a mortgagee for an account by the mortgagee in respect of the conduct of his powers of: (i) leasing the mortgaged land under section 129 of the Act ; (ii) selling the mortgaged land under section 132 to 134 of the Act ; (h) any application brought by one mortgagee against another mortgagee in a situation where land is subject to more than one mortgage and there is a dispute between the mortgages arising out of or connected to their respective mortgages; and (i) any other application arising out of or connected to a mortgage or mortgaged land.

provisions.title: particulars of claim for payment of moneys or possession of land
provisions.content: (1) Where the applicant claims as mortgagee payment of moneys secured by a mortgage of land or possession of such land, the particulars of claim shall include in addition to the matters specified in regulation 14, the following: (a) the date of the mortgage; (b) the identification of the land sought to be recovered; (c) where possession of the land is claimed, whether or not the land includes— (i) a dwelling house in which any person is in residence; (ii) any land in actual use for agriculture purposes or (iii) any land in actual use for pastoral purposes;

provisions.title: Mortgage claim-land in use and occupation
provisions.content: This regulation applies where a claimant as mortgagee possession of land to which regulation 9 applies. (2) Where the claimant’s claim is brought because of a failure to make the periodic payments due under the mortgage, the particulars of claim shall— [Please note: Numbering as in original.] (a) give details of all payments which have been missed altogether; (b) give details of any other payments required to be made as a term of the mortgage, such as insurance premiums, legal costs, default interest, penalties, administrative or other charges, together with any other sums claimed stating the nature and amount of each such charge, whether any payment is in arrear and whether or not it is included in the amount of any periodic payment; and (c) give such relevant information as is known by the claimant about the defendant’s circumstances. Part IV – Miscellaneous

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The following Regulations and Forms are hereby revoked. (a) the Land (Small Mortgages) Regulations, 2001 and [GN. No 75 of 2001] (b) Land Forms 40, 42, 43 and 53 of land (Forms) Regulations, 2001. [GN. definitions.definition: a person who makes a claim
an application for the possession of mortgaged land, including building and parts of buildings
the District Land and Housing Tribunal

an interest rate of a mortgage, terms and conditions of which empower the mortgagee on giving due notice to alter or vary the rate of interest payable with respect to the mortgage during the currency of the term of the mortgage.
